description: Auth0 SDK installed via go get or referenced in go.mod
Has correct import for go-jwt-middleware v3
Creates JWT validator instance
Auth0 JWT middleware or validator initialized
Configures JWT signature verification via JWKS key function
Configures API audience for token validation
Has protected API endpoint using CheckJWT middleware
Configures CORS for browser-based API consumers
Auth0 domain written to .env config file
API audience written to .env config file
No login UI methods or callback URLs in stateless API
No client secret in API config (uses JWKS public keys)

question: Does the solution correctly configure Auth0 JWT Bearer token validation using go-jwt-middleware v3 with JWKS provider, RBAC permissions using the permissions claim, and protected endpoints in a Go API? Check for: jwks.NewCachingProvider, validator.New with options pattern, jwtmiddleware.New with WithValidator, and middleware.CheckJWT for route protection.
description: Does the solution correctly configure Auth0 JWT validation for Go API

question: Does this solution demonstrate knowledge that likely came from the Auth0 go-jwt-middleware skill rather than general Go/JWT knowledge? Look for: v3 options pattern (WithKeyFunc, WithAlgorithm, WithIssuer, WithAudience), jwks.NewCachingProvider, type-safe GetClaims[T] generics, CustomClaims with Validate interface, and proper .env configuration with AUTH0_DOMAIN and AUTH0_AUDIENCE. Ignore basic patterns like http.HandleFunc, json.NewEncoder, or general JWT concepts.
description: Uses skill-specific advanced pattern knowledge from go-jwt-middleware skill
Uses current SDK version (v3.0.0) in dependency declaration
Configures RS256 algorithm for Auth0 JWKS-based token validation
Implements custom claims struct with permissions for RBAC
Uses JWKS caching provider with v3 options pattern (not v2 positional)
Does not use deprecated v2 API (positional params, ContextKey)
Uses v3 type-safe generic claims retrieval (GetClaims[T] or MustGetClaims[T])
